title: Add Same-Lap Field Percentiles And Local Traffic Features
summary: F1 strategy is relative, not absolute: undercut/overcut value depends on clean air, nearby rivals, and whether a driver is unusually slow or old-tyred versus the field on that exact lap.
rationale: F1 strategy is relative, not absolute: undercut/overcut value depends on clean air, nearby rivals, and whether a driver is unusually slow or old-tyred versus the field on that exact lap. Current code has some race-level medians, but not dense same-lap field-relative ranks or neighbor context.
implementation_hint: Within each `Year,Race,LapNumber`, compute percentile or robust-z features for `TyreLife`, `LapTime_Delta`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, and `Position`; also add local-neighbor gaps using cars at positions +/-1 to +/-2 and counts of rivals with fresher tyres or lower degradation nearby. Feed these into a tree model as relative-context specialists.
expected_effect: Should improve discrimination around real pit windows where absolute tyre age is ambiguous but relative pace/traffic pressure is decisive.
risk: Field-relative transforms can be noisy in laps with pit cycles or weather chaos, and careless use of future-derived ranking within a lap must stay strictly row-local.
